channel.title: Light Tower Group — NYC CRE Capital Markets Insights
channel.description: Data-driven capital markets analysis and building intelligence for NYC commercial real estate professionals.

item.title: Tuscan Village Leasing Shows Mixed-Use Capital Is Betting on Experience, Not Just Rent
item.description: Four new tenants including Whole Foods and Free People sign at Tuscan Village, a 170-acre mixed-use development near Boston. The leasing reveals capital is flowing to projects with experiential retail and residential density.

news:news.news:title: Tuscan Village Leasing Shows Mixed-Use Capital Is Betting on Experience, Not Just Rent
news:news.news:keywords: mixed-use, retail leasing, Tuscan Village, Whole Foods, capital markets

item.title: Bryant Park Grill Eviction Shows the Real Estate Value of a Prime Lease
item.description: Bryant Park Grill's eviction after a $26M sales year shows how park operators are maximizing revenue from prime retail assets, a trend reshaping urban concessions.

news:news.news:title: Bryant Park Grill Eviction Shows the Real Estate Value of a Prime Lease
news:news.news:keywords: retail, lease, eviction, urban real estate, concessions

item.title: Affordable Housing Deliveries Are Shrinking, But Still Above Pre-2020 Levels. That Gap Is the Story.
item.description: Yardi Matrix shows affordable housing deliveries shrinking but remaining above pre-2020 levels. The capital market signal is about pipeline sustainability, not demand weakness.

news:news.news:title: Affordable Housing Deliveries Are Shrinking, But Still Above Pre-2020 Levels. That Gap Is the Story.
news:news.news:keywords: affordable housing, Yardi Matrix, multifamily supply, development pipeline, capital markets

item.title: Detroit's 'Build, Baby, Build' Summit Signals Industrial CRE's Capital Inflection
item.description: At the Reindustrialize Summit, policy and capital converge on defense-driven manufacturing. The signal for industrial CRE: capital is concentrating around assets tied to national security supply chains.

news:news.news:title: Detroit's 'Build, Baby, Build' Summit Signals Industrial CRE's Capital Inflection
news:news.news:keywords: industrial, manufacturing, defense, capital flows, policy

item.title: Heiberger and Lev's Flatiron Townhouse Shows Boutique Condo Capital Is Betting on Scarcity, Not Scale
item.description: Buttonwood and Wheelhouse plan a three-unit Flatiron townhouse with a $20M sellout. The deal signals development capital is chasing scarcity and wealthy buyer demand for turnkey luxury.

news:news.news:title: Heiberger and Lev's Flatiron Townhouse Shows Boutique Condo Capital Is Betting on Scarcity, Not Scale
news:news.news:keywords: boutique development, New York City condos, townhouse market, luxury housing, development capital

item.title: A $38M Power Center Refi Shows Retail Debt Is Available for the Right Sponsor and Basis
item.description: Gantry secures $38M permanent loan for Mesa Grand power center in Phoenix. Full-term IO, cash-out proceeds, and an institutional balance sheet lender reveal where retail debt is flowing.

news:news.news:title: A $38M Power Center Refi Shows Retail Debt Is Available for the Right Sponsor and Basis
news:news.news:keywords: retail refinancing, power center, Phoenix, Gantry, balance sheet lender, interest-only loan, cash-out refinance

item.title: Fed Governor's Quiet Period Dinner: A Capital Markets Signal on Rate Policy and CRE Risk
item.description: A Trump-appointed Fed official spoke at a Bank of America dinner during the Fed's quiet period. The event signals potential policy leaks and uneven market access.

news:news.news:title: Fed Governor's Quiet Period Dinner: A Capital Markets Signal on Rate Policy and CRE Risk
news:news.news:keywords: Federal Reserve, quiet period, monetary policy, Bank of America, CRE capital markets

item.title: The Graffiti Towers Sale Is Stalled Because the City Sees the Billboard, Not the Building
item.description: Kali Chaudhuri's bid for LA's Graffiti Towers faces delay as city questions financing. The real prize: a 50,000-sf LED billboard worth tens of millions annually.

news:news.news:title: The Graffiti Towers Sale Is Stalled Because the City Sees the Billboard, Not the Building
news:news.news:keywords: Oceanwide Plaza, Graffiti Towers, KPC Square, Kali Chaudhuri, distressed real estate, billboard revenue, Los Angeles, bankruptcy sale, capital structure

item.title: Fed's Inflation Gauge Signals CRE's Cost of Capital Is Not Coming Down
item.description: A hotter PCE inflation reading reinforces Fed rate-hike expectations, keeping CRE debt costs elevated and refinancing windows narrow for 2026 maturities.

news:news.news:title: Fed's Inflation Gauge Signals CRE's Cost of Capital Is Not Coming Down
news:news.news:keywords: Federal Reserve, inflation, interest rates, refinancing risk, cost of capital

item.title: The $87.5 Million Escrow Gap: How Title and Law Firms Became the Engine of CRE Fraud
item.description: A federal lawsuit alleges Riverside Abstract and Nussbaum Lowinger orchestrated $87.5M in escrow fraud through sham flips, inflated valuations, and concealed kickbacks. The case exposes a critical vulnerability in CRE capital flows.

news:news.news:title: The $87.5 Million Escrow Gap: How Title and Law Firms Became the Engine of CRE Fraud
news:news.news:keywords: fraud, escrow, title, law firm, valuation, leverage, 1031 exchange, Riverside Abstract, Nussbaum Lowinger

item.title: NYC's $1B Preservation Program: Capital Is Not the Constraint, Timing Is
item.description: NYC's $1B Supportive Preservation Program targets 30,000 units. The real signal is how public capital is stepping in where private debt cannot yet refinance.

news:news.news:title: NYC's $1B Preservation Program: Capital Is Not the Constraint, Timing Is
news:news.news:keywords: affordable housing, New York City, HPD, preservation, public capital, supportive housing

item.title: Aby Rosen's $81.5M Sale Shows the Price of Empty Trophy Assets
item.description: Aby Rosen sells 281 Park Ave South for $81.5M, a 40% discount from 2022 ask. The empty landmark shows how vacancy and carry costs reset pricing for even the most famous buildings.

news:news.news:title: Aby Rosen's $81.5M Sale Shows the Price of Empty Trophy Assets
news:news.news:keywords: Aby Rosen, RFR, 281 Park Avenue South, New York office, vacancy, basis reset, liquidity

item.title: Geopolitics Gave CRE Borrowers a 5-Basis-Point Gift. The Fed Just Took It Back.
item.description: US-Iran ceasefire drops 30-year mortgage rate to 6.47%, but Fed dot plot signals year-end rate of 3.8%. CRE borrowers face a narrow, volatile window for refinancing.

news:news.news:title: Geopolitics Gave CRE Borrowers a 5-Basis-Point Gift. The Fed Just Took It Back.
news:news.news:keywords: mortgage rates, Treasury yields, Federal Reserve, refinancing, commercial real estate

item.title: Affinius's $1B Data Center Fund Shows Institutional Capital Is Chasing Power, Not Real Estate
item.description: Affinius nears $1bn for its first data center fund, backed by La Caisse. The raise signals institutional capital is prioritizing power access and infrastructure over traditional real estate risk.

news:news.news:title: Affinius's $1B Data Center Fund Shows Institutional Capital Is Chasing Power, Not Real Estate
news:news.news:keywords: data centers, Affinius, La Caisse, institutional capital, infrastructure

item.title: Capital Group's $210M Buy Shows Office Ownership Is a Rent Decision, Not a Market Bet
item.description: Capital Group buys its 55-story DTLA HQ for $210M. The deal reveals that for creditworthy tenants, owning is now cheaper than leasing. A capital markets signal, not a market bet.

news:news.news:title: Capital Group's $210M Buy Shows Office Ownership Is a Rent Decision, Not a Market Bet
news:news.news:keywords: Capital Group, office, Los Angeles, owner-user, Brookfield, special servicing, vertical campus

item.title: Apollo CREF's Dissolution Shows Public REITs Can't Absorb Concentrated Losses
item.description: Apollo CREF dissolves after $82M write-off on 111 West 57th Street and other concentrated losses. The $9B portfolio sale to Athene reveals public REITs' inability to absorb illiquid credit risk.

news:news.news:title: Apollo CREF's Dissolution Shows Public REITs Can't Absorb Concentrated Losses
news:news.news:keywords: Apollo, CREF, mortgage REIT, liquidation, 111 West 57th Street, Athene, commercial real estate lending, credit losses

item.title: Beazer's $400M Refi Raises Dream Finders' Takeover Cost by $53 Million
item.description: Beazer Homes refinanced $400M in notes, adding a $53M make-whole penalty that raises Dream Finders' acquisition cost by ~$2 per share.

news:news.news:title: Beazer's $400M Refi Raises Dream Finders' Takeover Cost by $53 Million
news:news.news:keywords: Beazer Homes, Dream Finders Homes, M&A, refinancing, change of control

item.title: Brookfield's Ground-Lease JV: A Liquidity Trade, Not a Vote of Confidence
item.description: Brookfield takes a non-controlling stake in a Safehold ground-lease portfolio with a seven-year buyback option. The deal reveals the cost of public market capital and the liquidity trade beneath the headline.

news:news.news:title: Brookfield's Ground-Lease JV: A Liquidity Trade, Not a Vote of Confidence
news:news.news:keywords: Brookfield, Safehold, ground lease, joint venture, liquidity, public REIT, capital markets

item.title: Warsh's First Fed Meeting Confirms CRE's Higher-for-Longer Reality
item.description: Fed holds rates at 3.5%-3.75% as Warsh's first meeting signals no 2026 cuts. CRE faces $875B in maturing loans with refinancing pressure unchanged.

news:news.news:title: Warsh's First Fed Meeting Confirms CRE's Higher-for-Longer Reality
news:news.news:keywords: Federal Reserve, interest rates, Kevin Warsh, CRE refinancing, monetary policy

item.title: Yellowstone's $480M Loan Shows Conversion Capital Is Flowing to the Right Sponsor and Basis
item.description: Yellowstone secures $480M construction loan from Madison Realty Capital for 1740 Broadway office-to-residential conversion. The deal reveals how private credit is pricing conversion risk and sponsor credibility.

news:news.news:title: Yellowstone's $480M Loan Shows Conversion Capital Is Flowing to the Right Sponsor and Basis
news:news.news:keywords: Yellowstone Real Estate Investments, Madison Realty Capital, 1740 Broadway, office-to-residential conversion, construction financing

item.title: The $5 Billion Mirage: Inside New York City's Municipal Shell Game and the Imminent 2028 Fiscal Cliff
item.description: New York City's FY 2027 budget removes a 9.5% property tax hike, but $5.07 billion of one-time fiscal maneuvers point to a larger FY 2028 budget gap and rising risk for CRE underwriting.

news:news.news:title: The $5 Billion Mirage: Inside New York City's Municipal Shell Game and the Imminent 2028 Fiscal Cliff
news:news.news:keywords: New York City budget, property taxes, municipal finance, commercial real estate, CRE underwriting, fiscal cliff, capital markets

item.title: Cord Meyer's Bay Terrace Build Shows As-of-Right Density Is the Cheapest Capital in Queens
item.description: Cord Meyer Development starts 145 rental units at Bay Terrace Shopping Center in Bayside, Queens. As-of-right zoning and existing land control reduce execution risk, making construction debt available where speculative projects struggle.

news:news.news:title: Cord Meyer's Bay Terrace Build Shows As-of-Right Density Is the Cheapest Capital in Queens
news:news.news:keywords: Cord Meyer Development, Bay Terrace, Bayside Queens, as-of-right development, construction financing, retail-to-residential, STUDIO V Architecture

item.title: Income Insurance's Pivot Shows Insurers Are Rethinking Real Estate's Role in a Higher-Cost World
item.description: Income Insurance explores credit strategies as higher costs reshape real estate returns. The shift signals insurers are reallocating capital away from direct property exposure.

news:news.news:title: Income Insurance's Pivot Shows Insurers Are Rethinking Real Estate's Role in a Higher-Cost World
news:news.news:keywords: Income Insurance, insurance capital, real estate strategy, credit, capital allocation

item.title: Iran Conflict Could Push Mortgage Rates Past 7% Into Late 2026, Threatening CRE Refinancing
item.description: Iran conflict could push mortgage rates 0.375%-0.435% above 6.75% into late 2026, threatening CRE refinancing windows and compressing borrower timelines.

news:news.news:title: Iran Conflict Could Push Mortgage Rates Past 7% Into Late 2026, Threatening CRE Refinancing
news:news.news:keywords: mortgage rates, Iran conflict, refinancing risk, commercial real estate, Federal Reserve

item.title: The Real Estate Portal War Is Now a Capital Markets Story
item.description: NYT coverage of Zillow and Compass battles signals regulatory risk that could reshape platform economics, listing access, and consumer trust.

news:news.news:title: The Real Estate Portal War Is Now a Capital Markets Story
news:news.news:keywords: Zillow, Compass, Anywhere, antitrust, regulatory risk

item.title: Data Center Construction Is the Only Thing Keeping U.S. Building Activity From Stalling
item.description: Data center and advanced manufacturing construction is propping up U.S. building activity. Multifamily spending is shrinking. Turner Construction sees data center revenue doubling by 2030.

news:news.news:title: Data Center Construction Is the Only Thing Keeping U.S. Building Activity From Stalling
news:news.news:keywords: data centers, multifamily, construction spending, capital allocation, AI infrastructure

item.title: Bond Selloff on Iran Strikes Puts CRE Refinancing Under a Tighter Clock
item.description: Bond yields surge as Israel-Iran strikes stoke inflation fears. For CRE borrowers, the window to refinance floating-rate debt is narrowing as rate-cut expectations vanish.

news:news.news:title: Bond Selloff on Iran Strikes Puts CRE Refinancing Under a Tighter Clock
news:news.news:keywords: bond yields, inflation, refinancing risk, floating-rate debt, Federal Reserve

item.title: Soloviev's $1.8B Refi Shows Trophy Office Debt Is Available, but Only for the 100% Leased
item.description: Soloviev Group refinances 9 West 57th St with $1.8B from Bank of America. The tower is 100% leased at record rents. The deal shows trophy office debt is available, but only for assets with no vacancy risk.

news:news.news:title: Soloviev's $1.8B Refi Shows Trophy Office Debt Is Available, but Only for the 100% Leased
news:news.news:keywords: Soloviev Group, 9 West 57th Street, Bank of America, office refinancing, trophy office

item.title: Private Credit's Institutional Calm Belies Retail Panic — And That Gap Matters
item.description: Arcmont CEO Anthony Fobel says institutional investors still trust private credit despite retail outflows, as default rates stay low. The gap reveals who controls CRE's liquidity.

news:news.news:title: Private Credit's Institutional Calm Belies Retail Panic — And That Gap Matters
news:news.news:keywords: private credit, institutional investors, retail capital, default rates, liquidity

item.title: Blackstone’s $2B Fund-Stake Sale Is a Liquidity Trade, Not a Conviction Call
item.description: Blackstone seeks to sell $2B of private fund stakes via bonds. The move signals liquidity demand, not bearishness, and tests investor appetite for structured risk.

news:news.news:title: Blackstone’s $2B Fund-Stake Sale Is a Liquidity Trade, Not a Conviction Call
news:news.news:keywords: Blackstone, private equity, fund stakes, liquidity, capital markets, structured finance

item.title: San Pedro's Price Jump Is Not a Renaissance. It Is a Basis Trade on LA's Housing Shortage.
item.description: San Pedro home prices rose from $700K to $1M in 10 years. Trammell Crow and Jerico Development are betting the discount to Santa Monica, not the waterfront, drives demand.

news:news.news:title: San Pedro's Price Jump Is Not a Renaissance. It Is a Basis Trade on LA's Housing Shortage.
news:news.news:keywords: San Pedro, Los Angeles, Trammell Crow, Jerico Development, West Harbor, basis trade, housing shortage, waterfront development, multifamily, capital flows

item.title: Soho's 144 Spring Street: A Design Revision That Reveals the Cost of Land and Time
item.description: City Urban Realty's new proposal for 144 Spring Street in Soho replaces a 2016-approved design. The shift from glass to precast concrete reveals rising construction costs, patient land, and the economics of small-scale development.

news:news.news:title: Soho's 144 Spring Street: A Design Revision That Reveals the Cost of Land and Time
news:news.news:keywords: Soho, development economics, construction costs, land basis, City Urban Realty, New York City development

item.title: Mamdani's YIMBY-Socialist Housing Plan Splits the Capital Stack: New Supply Gets Market Pricing, Old Stock Gets Political Control
item.description: Mayor Mamdani's housing plan unites YIMBYs and socialists by separating new construction (market liberal) from existing stock (aggressive tenant protections). Capital markets implications for development financing and rent-stabilized asset values.

news:news.news:title: Mamdani's YIMBY-Socialist Housing Plan Splits the Capital Stack: New Supply Gets Market Pricing, Old Stock Gets Political Control
news:news.news:keywords: Zohran Mamdani, YIMBY, socialist housing policy, New York City housing, capital stack bifurcation

item.title: Studio 54's $100M Renovation Shows Theaters Must Sell Air Rights to Survive
item.description: Roundabout Theatre's $100M Studio 54 renovation is funded by selling air rights. This reveals how cultural venues monetize unrealized density to finance capital needs.

news:news.news:title: Studio 54's $100M Renovation Shows Theaters Must Sell Air Rights to Survive
news:news.news:keywords: air rights, development rights, theater renovation, Roundabout Theatre, Studio 54, NYC zoning, cultural capital

item.title: Cord Meyer's Bay Terrace Build Shows Retail Owners Are Becoming Developers to Unlock Land Value
item.description: Cord Meyer Development adds 145 rental units to Bay Terrace Shopping Center in Bayside, Queens. The project reveals how retail owners are monetizing excess land to fund property upgrades and capture multifamily demand.

news:news.news:title: Cord Meyer's Bay Terrace Build Shows Retail Owners Are Becoming Developers to Unlock Land Value
news:news.news:keywords: Cord Meyer Development, Bay Terrace, Bayside, Queens, retail-to-residential, as-of-right development, STUDIO V Architecture, multifamily construction, parking lot redevelopment, New York City development

item.title: Ingredion's Tate & Lyle Bid Shows Private Capital Is Buying London Listing Discount
item.description: Ingredion's £2.7B bid for Tate & Lyle reveals how public market discounting is fueling take-private deals. A capital markets signal on valuation, liquidity, and exit strategy.

news:news.news:title: Ingredion's Tate & Lyle Bid Shows Private Capital Is Buying London Listing Discount
news:news.news:keywords: M&A, take-private, London Stock Exchange, valuation gap, Ingredion, Tate & Lyle, capital markets

item.title: Manhattan Trophy Office Space Is Running Out. That Changes the Capital Calculus.
item.description: Cushman & Wakefield reports direct availability at 32 trophy towers fell to 4.4%. The capital implication: pricing power is returning to landlords, but only at the top of the market.

news:news.news:title: Manhattan Trophy Office Space Is Running Out. That Changes the Capital Calculus.
news:news.news:keywords: Manhattan office, trophy towers, leasing demand, Cushman & Wakefield, CBRE

item.title: ECB Rate Hike Signals Global CRE Repricing Has Further to Run
item.description: ECB rate hike amid Iran war tightens euro-zone financial conditions, extending the refinancing window for European commercial real estate.

news:news.news:title: ECB Rate Hike Signals Global CRE Repricing Has Further to Run
news:news.news:keywords: ECB, interest rates, European CRE, refinancing risk, global tightening

item.title: Hedge Fund Crowding Poses a New Risk for CRE Credit Markets
item.description: Friday's equities rout renews concerns about crowded trades. For CRE capital markets, the risk is not the selloff itself but the liquidity withdrawal it could trigger.

news:news.news:title: Hedge Fund Crowding Poses a New Risk for CRE Credit Markets
news:news.news:keywords: hedge funds, crowded trades, liquidity risk, credit markets, systemic risk

item.title: NY AG's Compass Probe: The Real Antitrust Risk Is Capital Market Access
item.description: NY AG probes Compass over antitrust after Anywhere merger. The real risk is not divestiture but capital market access and platform valuation.

news:news.news:title: NY AG's Compass Probe: The Real Antitrust Risk Is Capital Market Access
news:news.news:keywords: Compass, antitrust, New York Attorney General, residential brokerage, capital markets

item.title: Nippon Life's $10B Blackstone Bet Shows Insurance Capital Is Chasing Yield Through Private Credit
item.description: Nippon Life commits ¥1.5 trillion ($10B) to Blackstone for private credit and real estate. The deal reveals insurance capital's hunger for yield and the rise of asset-manager intermediation.

news:news.news:title: Nippon Life's $10B Blackstone Bet Shows Insurance Capital Is Chasing Yield Through Private Credit
news:news.news:keywords: Nippon Life, Blackstone, private credit, Japan, insurance capital, real estate debt, institutional allocation

item.title: Compass Stock Drop: Antitrust Risk Is Now a Capital Markets Liability
item.description: Compass stock fell 13% on NY AG antitrust probe. The market is pricing regulatory risk into CRE platform valuations, not just fundamentals.

news:news.news:title: Compass Stock Drop: Antitrust Risk Is Now a Capital Markets Liability
news:news.news:keywords: Compass, antitrust, NY AG, stock price, regulatory risk

item.title: Rockrose's $70M Freddie Mac Refi Shows Agency Debt Is Buying Time, Not Solving the Cycle
item.description: Rockrose Development secures $70M Freddie Mac refi for 100 Jane Street. The deal shows agency debt is available for stabilized assets in supply-constrained submarkets, but it buys time, not a cycle exit.

news:news.news:title: Rockrose's $70M Freddie Mac Refi Shows Agency Debt Is Buying Time, Not Solving the Cycle
news:news.news:keywords: Freddie Mac, Rockrose Development, multifamily refinancing, agency lending, West Village

item.title: The $1 Trillion Risk Transfer: Banks Are Lending, but Someone Else Is Holding the Risk
item.description: Banks transferred €905B in loan risk via SRTs in 2025, up 26%. Asset managers and insurers now dominate, reshaping CRE capital availability and systemic risk.

news:news.news:title: The $1 Trillion Risk Transfer: Banks Are Lending, but Someone Else Is Holding the Risk
news:news.news:keywords: SRT, bank credit risk, asset managers, insurers, commercial real estate, capital markets, private credit

item.title: TPG's $2B ECHO Buy Shows Institutional Capital Still Wants Grocery-Anchored Retail
item.description: TPG, PSP Investments, La Caisse, and Norges Bank acquire ECHO Realty for ~$2B. The deal signals institutional appetite for grocery-anchored retail with defensive cash flow and credible operators.

news:news.news:title: TPG's $2B ECHO Buy Shows Institutional Capital Still Wants Grocery-Anchored Retail
news:news.news:keywords: TPG Real Estate, ECHO Realty, grocery-anchored retail, institutional capital, retail acquisitions

item.title: Jobs Data Resets the CRE Rate Timeline: Hikes Are Back on the Table
item.description: June jobs report fuels Fed hike speculation, extending expensive debt costs for CRE owners facing 2026 maturities. Liquidity window narrows.

news:news.news:title: Jobs Data Resets the CRE Rate Timeline: Hikes Are Back on the Table
news:news.news:keywords: Federal Reserve, interest rates, jobs report, refinancing risk, capital markets

item.title: Private Credit's Redemption Caps Signal a Liquidity Reality Check
item.description: Blackstone caps BCRED withdrawals at 5% after $4.4B in requests. The move signals private credit's liquidity mismatch and what it means for CRE capital flows.

news:news.news:title: Private Credit's Redemption Caps Signal a Liquidity Reality Check
news:news.news:keywords: private credit, redemption caps, Blackstone, BCRED, liquidity risk, commercial real estate, capital markets

item.title: Cohen Brothers' $114M Office Sale to ESRT Shows the Price of Liquidity
item.description: Cohen Brothers sells two Manhattan office towers to Empire State Realty Trust for $114M, or ~$70/sf. The price reveals the bid for repriced office assets and the cost of liquidity.

news:news.news:title: Cohen Brothers' $114M Office Sale to ESRT Shows the Price of Liquidity
news:news.news:keywords: Cohen Brothers Realty, Empire State Realty Trust, Manhattan office, office sale, liquidity

item.title: Kohan's 76% Discount Buy Shows Office Repricing Has a Floor, Not a Ceiling
item.description: Kohan Retail Investment Group buys 1.5 MSF Chicago office tower for $137M, a 76% discount from 2006. The deal signals office repricing is clearing at distressed basis, not recovery.

news:news.news:title: Kohan's 76% Discount Buy Shows Office Repricing Has a Floor, Not a Ceiling
news:news.news:keywords: office, Chicago, Kohan, repricing, distressed

item.title: Alterra IOS's $244M Blackstone Loan Shows Private Credit Is Betting on Niche Industrial Scale
item.description: Alterra IOS secures $244M from Blackstone for 37 IOS sites. The nonrecourse loan signals private credit's appetite for niche industrial scale and sponsor quality.

news:news.news:title: Alterra IOS's $244M Blackstone Loan Shows Private Credit Is Betting on Niche Industrial Scale
news:news.news:keywords: Alterra IOS, Blackstone, industrial outdoor storage, private credit, acquisition financing, BREDS
